item.title: 聊天机器人的兴起：数字营销的新时代已经到来！ 🤖 🤖 | The Rise of Chatbots: A New Era of Digital Marketing Has Arrived! 🤖
item.description:
怎么了，聊天机器人爱好者？ 是你的人 Uttam Kumar Dash 带着数字营销人员文摘来找你。 今天，我为您带来了一些关于聊天机器人技术领域的激动人心的见解。

您是否知道聊天机器人正在以前所未有的速度接管市场，超越所有增长预期？

从大企业到小型在线商店，甚至地方政府也加入了聊天机器人的行列。 那么为何不？ 凭借节省成本和无限潜力，聊天机器人是各种企业的宝贵资产。

但是等等，还有更多！

现在，聊天机器人不仅仅用于基本的客户交互。 他们提供个性化的响应、改进的客户服务和 24/7 可用性。 此外，它们还可以通过实时测量情绪等方式帮助公司更好地了解客户的需求和偏好。

随着社交媒体和消息传递应用程序的兴起，消费者正在寻求即时满足和个性化体验，而聊天机器人是完美的解决方案。

事实上，数字不言而喻。

您是否知道聊天机器人可以将客户参与度提高多达 60%？ 这不仅仅是一种趋势，这是一场革命。 如果您还没有加入，您将错失一个以有意义的方式与您的观众建立联系的巨大机会。

但事情是这样的——并不是所有的聊天机器人都是生而平等的。 因此，如果您想在竞争中脱颖而出并加入这个数字营销的新时代，那就让我们加入吧！

每个人都在谈论的这些聊天机器人到底是什么？

聊天机器人是通过文本或语音交互模仿人类对话的软件应用程序。 目标是模拟自然对话。

早在 1950 年，艾伦图灵就提出了著名的图灵测试，以此作为衡量计算机程序在实时对话中模仿人类的能力的一种方法。

现在，随着人工智能的所有这些进步，聊天机器人变得更加复杂。 他们使用深度学习和自然语言处理来保持真实的对话。 那么，这些天聊天机器人在哪里使用？

好吧，一个主要领域是客户服务和支持。 虚拟助手正变得越来越普遍。 此外，各行各业的公司都在利用生成式人工智能技术推动这一领域的先进发展。

聊天机器人如何改变数字营销格局

让我们看看聊天机器人如何改变数字营销游戏规则的一些真实例子。

客户分析

首先，客户分析。 由 Conversational AI 提供支持的聊天机器人可以自动收集有关客户行为的见解和数据点、跟踪购买模式并分析习惯。

有了这些信息，企业可以更好地回答客户问题、追加销售其他产品，甚至预测未来的客户需求。

这就是聊天机器人的用武之地。

他们实时吸引潜在客户，帮助将潜在客户转化为忠诚客户。 通过提供有针对性的消息、特惠、优惠券和其他优惠，企业可以让客户回头光顾。

领先一代

由对话式 AI 提供支持的聊天机器人可以分析大量数据，帮助企业在客户中找到潜在的潜在客户。 在招聘方面，这有助于浏览成堆的简历以找到理想的工作候选人。

电子邮件营销

电子邮件营销是聊天机器人可以产生巨大影响的另一个领域。 企业可以通过根据用户行为触发电子邮件并使用相关内容改进营销活动来个性化电子邮件营销。

结果？？？

更多的转化和更好的投资回报率！ 社交媒体推广

这也得到了聊天机器人的推动。 他们可以回答客户查询、提供个性化内容并自动进行客户拓展。 这一切都是在保持活跃的社交媒体存在的同时，没有相关的时间或成本。

个性化营销

个性化营销是聊天机器人改变游戏规则的另一个领域。 通过使用从过去的交互和后端系统收集的信息，聊天机器人可以提供个性化的体验。

这些还可以推荐符合用户兴趣和需求的产品。 研究表明，对品牌有情感联系的人更有可能购买其产品并成为其拥护者。

由对话式 AI 提供支持的聊天机器人为品牌提供了一种更“人性化”的方式来与潜在客户和客户互动。

因此，毫无疑问，聊天机器人正在极大地改变格局。

聊天机器人好处的必然清单

以下是使用聊天机器人的一些流行但被低估的好处：

  • 个性化和有针对性的互动
  • 提高效率和节约成本
  • 能够处理重复性任务并释放人力资源
  • 改进数据收集和分析
  • 能够处理大量请求和查询
  • 通过始终如一的优质服务提高客户满意度和忠诚度

被低估的营销优势：

  • 增加潜在客户生成和转化率。
  • 提升品牌形象和美誉度。
  • 通过使用多语言支持能够处理多种语言和文化。
  • 收集客户反馈和见解的机会。
  • 通过基于聊天机器人的服务获得新收入来源的潜力。
  • 通过减少工作量并专注于更复杂的任务来提高员工满意度。
  • 与 CRM 等系统集成，实现无缝用户体验。
  • 协助处理危机并与客户沟通。
  • 实施聊天机器人时的未来挑战和注意事项
  • 虽然聊天机器人肯定可以彻底改变企业与客户互动的方式，但它并非没有障碍。

自然语言处理能力

首先，我们需要考虑聊天机器人的技术局限性。 虽然可以对它们进行编程以理解 NL 并做出相应的反应，但它们并不完美。

聊天机器人可能难以理解不在其数据库中的复杂查询或语言，从而导致令人沮丧的客户体验。

因此，继续完善聊天机器人的 NLP 功能以确保它能够处理范围广泛的查询非常重要。

聊天机器人的设计需要考虑到用户。 您需要确保他们能够理解您的机器人并与之互动。 这意味着要考虑客户可能使用的不同语言、俚语和口音。

您需要考虑聊天机器人实施的技术方面。 说话的语气

另一个挑战是保持聊天机器人的语调。 虽然为您的聊天机器人提供品牌特定的声音很重要，但同样重要的是要确保语气不会给人留下机械或不真诚的印象。

聊天机器人应该能够在保持高效的同时听起来友好、乐于助人和善解人意。

人为监督

企业经常忽视的一个考虑因素是人为监督的重要性。 虽然聊天机器人可以处理大量请求，但在某些情况下它们仍然需要人工干预。

例如，如果客户生气或沮丧，聊天机器人可能无法像人类客户服务代表那样处理这种情况。

重要的是要有一个系统，聊天机器人可以在必要时将客户转移给人类代表。

隐私和安全

隐私和安全是另一个问题。 客户总是通过聊天机器人提供个人信息。 因此，确保数据的安全存储和传输非常重要。

企业应与开发人员合作实施安全措施。 加密和双因素身份验证等措施可保护客户数据。

聊天机器人再次让数字营销变得有趣

正如 David Perlmutter 博士曾经说过的，

而这正是聊天机器人所代表的——进步。

他们正在改变客户与我们业务互动的方式，提高效率，并提供有价值的数据见解。

所以，伙计们，不要在这个数字营销的新时代落伍了。 拥抱聊天机器人的力量，将您的业务提升到一个新的水平。

记住，伙计们，未来就在这里，掌握在你们手中。 这很令人兴奋，而且应该如此！

感谢您的阅读！ 不要忘记订阅我的时事通讯 👉🏽 Nerd Experience ，以获取有关数字营销最新趋势和创新的更多令人兴奋的更新。

我很想听听您对这个话题的看法！ 请随时在下面的评论中分享您的意见。

这篇文章最初发布在我的 Substack 个人资料“数字营销文摘”中。



原文:

What’s up, chatbot enthusiasts? It’s your guy Uttam Kumar Dash coming at you with The Digital Marketers' Digest. Today, I’ve come to you with some exciting insights into the realm of chatbot technology.

Do you know that chatbots are taking over at an unprecedented pace, surpassing all growth expectations?

From big businesses to small online stores, even local governments are jumping on the chatbot bandwagon. And why not? With cost savings and endless potential, chatbots are a valuable asset for businesses of all kinds.

But wait, there’s more!

Now, chatbots aren’t just for basic customer interactions. They offer personalized responses, improved customer service, and 24/7 availability. Plus, they can help companies better understand customer needs and preferences by measuring sentiment in real-time, and so on.

With the rise of social media and messaging apps, consumers are looking for instant gratification and personalized experiences, and chatbots are the perfect solution for that.

In fact, the numbers speak for themselves.

Did you know that chatbots can increase customer engagement by up to 60%? And that 80% of businesses plan to use chatbots by 2024?

That’s not just a trend, that’s a revolution. And if you’re not on board yet, you’re missing out on a huge opportunity to connect with your audience in a meaningful way.

But here’s the thing — not all chatbots are created equal. So, if you want to stand out from the competition and join in this new era of digital marketing, let’s jump right in!

What the Heck are these Chatbots Everyone’s Talking About?

Chatbots are software apps that mimic human conversation through text or voice interactions. The goal is to simulate a natural conversation.

Back in 1950, Alan Turing proposed the famous Turing test as a way of measuring a computer program’s ability to impersonate a human being in real-time conversation.

Joseph Weizenbaum’s program ELIZA, which was published in 1966, was one of the earliest examples of a chatbot that was able to fool users into thinking they were talking to a real human.

ELIZA used the recognition of clue words or phrases in order to generate responses that seemed intelligent, even though the processing involved was fairly simple.

The term “ChatterBot” was first coined in 1994 by Michael Mauldin, who created the first Verbot. From there came the word “Chatbot”.

Now, with all these advancements in AI, chatbots have become more sophisticated. They use deep learning and natural language processing to maintain authentic conversations. OpenAI’s ChatGPT is now the most trending example. Bing Chat and Google’s Bard are alternatives built upon large language models.

So, where are chatbots being used these days?

Well, one major area is customer service and support. Virtual assistants are becoming more and more common. Moreover, companies from a wide range of industries are utilizing generative AI technologies to power advanced developments in this area.

How Chatbots are Changing the Digital Marketing Landscape

Let’s see some real-life examples of how chatbots are changing the game in digital marketing.

Customer Analysis

First off, customer analysis. Chatbots powered by Conversational AI can automatically gather insights and data points on customer behavior, track purchasing patterns, and analyze habits.

With this information, businesses can better answer customer questions, upsell additional products, and even predict future customer needs.

Research from InsideSales.com shows that lapses between form submission and marketing or sales contact have a direct impact on whether leads are converted into customers. That’s where chatbots come in.

They engage leads in real time, helping to convert potential customers into loyal ones. By providing targeted messages, special deals, coupons, and other offers, businesses can keep customers coming back for more.

Lead Generation

Chatbots powered by Conversational AI can analyze vast amounts of data to help businesses find potential leads among their customers. And when it comes to recruiting, this helps to scan through stacks of resumes to find the ideal candidate for the job.

Email Marketing

Email marketing is another area where chatbots can make a huge impact. Businesses can personalize email marketing by triggering emails based on user behavior and improving campaigns with relevant content.

The result???

More conversions and better ROI! 🎉

Social Media Outreach

This is also getting a boost from chatbots. They can answer customer queries, deliver personalized content, and automate customer outreach. And it is all while maintaining an active social media presence without the associated time or cost.

Personalized Marketing

Personalized marketing is another area where chatbots are changing the game. By using the information gathered from past interactions and back-end systems, chatbots can provide personalized experiences.

These can also suggest products that match a user’s interests and needs. Research shows that people who feel an emotional connection to a brand are more likely to purchase its products and become advocates.

Chatbots powered by Conversational AI provide a more “human” way for brands to engage with prospects and customers.

So, there is no doubt that chatbots are changing the landscape in a big way.

The Inevitable List of Chatbot Benefits

Here are some popular and underrated benefits of using chatbots:

  • 24/7 availability and instant response time
  • Personalized and targeted interactions
  • Increased efficiency and cost savings
  • Ability to handle repetitive tasks and free up human resources
  • Improved data collection and analysis
  • Ability to handle a large volume of requests and queries
  • Enhanced customer satisfaction and loyalty through consistent, high-quality service

Underrated marketing benefits:

  • Increased lead generation and conversion rates.
  • Improved brand image and reputation.
  • Ability to handle multiple languages and cultures by using multilingual support.
  • Opportunity to gather customer feedback and insights.
  • Potential for new revenue streams through chatbot-based services.
  • Improved employee satisfaction through reduced workload and focus on more complex tasks.
  • Integrations with systems like CRM for seamless user experience.
  • Assistance in crisis management and communication with customers.
  • Future Challenges and Considerations While Implementing Chatbots
  • Though, chatbots can certainly revolutionize the way businesses interact with customers, it’s not without its hurdles.

NLP Capabilities

Firstly, we need to consider the technical limitations of chatbots. While they can be programmed to understand NL and respond accordingly, they are not perfect.

Chatbots may struggle with understanding complex queries or language that is not in their database, leading to frustrating customer experiences.

So, it’s important to continue refining the chatbot’s NLP capabilities to ensure that it can handle a wide range of queries.

Chatbots need to be designed with the user in mind. And you need to ensure that they can understand and interact with your bot. This means taking into account the different languages, slang, and accents your customers may use.

You need to consider the technical aspects of chatbot implementation. APIs like ChatGPT can be integrated with your chatbot to provide more accurate and personalized responses.

Tone of Voice

Another challenge is maintaining the chatbot’s tone of voice. While it’s important to give your chatbot a brand-specific voice, it’s equally important to ensure that the tone doesn’t come across as robotic or insincere.

The chatbot should be able to sound friendly, helpful, and empathetic while still being efficient.

Human Oversight

One consideration that businesses often overlook is the importance of human oversight. While chatbots can handle a large volume of requests, they still require human intervention in certain situations.

For example, if a customer is angry or frustrated, a chatbot may not be able to handle the situation as well as a human customer service representative.

It’s important to have a system in place where the chatbot can transfer the customer to a human representative when necessary.

Privacy and Security

Privacy and security is another concern. Customers always provide personal information through chatbots. So, it is important to ensure that the data is stored and transmitted securely.

Businesses should work with developers to implement security measures. Measures such as encryption and two-factor authentication to protect customer data.

Chatbots are Making Digital Marketing Fun Again

As Dr. David Perlmutter once said,

“Strive for progress, not perfection.”

And that’s precisely what chatbots represent — progress.

They’re transforming the way customers are interacting with our businesses, improving efficiency, and providing valuable data insights.

So, guys, don’t get left behind in this new era of digital marketing. Embrace the power of chatbots and take your business to the next level.

Remember, folks, the future is here and in your hands. It’s exciting, and it should be!

Thank you for reading! Don’t forget to subscribe to my newsletter 👉🏽 Nerd Experience for more exciting updates on the latest trends and innovations in digital marketing.

I’d love to hear your thoughts on this topic! Feel free to share your opinions in the comments below.

This post was originally published on my Substack profile, ‘The Digital Marketing Digest’.
item.author: ukdrahul

item.title: DevOps 简介：你需要知道的一切 | Introduction to Devops: Everything you need to know
item.description:
在本文中，我们将涵盖您需要了解的有关 DevOps 的所有信息，包括其历史、原则、优势和最佳实践。

传统开发实践的特点是发布周期长、团队孤立以及开发人员和运营人员之间缺乏协作。 这导致软件交付过程缓慢且容易出错，经常出现延迟和错误。 这些原则是：

文化：DevOps 强调协作、沟通和持续改进的文化。 团队共同努力打破孤岛，并为交付软件建立共同的主人翁意识和责任感。

自动化： DevOps 依靠自动化来简化软件交付流程并减少手动错误。 自动化测试、部署和监控可帮助团队更快地交付质量更高的软件。

测量：DevOps 团队使用数据和指标来测量其软件交付过程的性能并确定需要改进的领域。 这包括交货时间、部署频率和平均恢复时间等指标。

共享：DevOps 团队共享知识、工具和流程，以加速学习并提高效率。 这包括创建共享知识库、使用通用工具和平台以及共享最佳实践。

持续改进：DevOps 是一个强调持续改进的迭代过程。 团队一直在寻找优化软件交付流程和减少浪费的方法。

这些好处包括：

更快的上市时间：DevOps 使团队能够通过简化软件交付流程和缩短周期时间来更快地交付软件。

加强协作：DevOps 将开发和运营团队聚集在一起，为交付软件创造协作和共享所有权的文化。

更有效地利用资源：DevOps 通过简化软件交付流程和自动化重复性任务来减少浪费。

更好的客户满意度：DevOps 使组织能够更快、更可靠地交付满足客户需求和期望的软件。

为了充分利用 DevOps，组织应该遵循一组最佳实践。 这些做法包括：

自动化一切：尽可能多地自动化软件交付过程，包括测试、部署和监控。

使用版本控制：使用版本控制系统来管理代码更改并更有效地协作。

实施持续集成和交付：使用持续集成和交付来自动化软件交付流程并缩短周期时间。

监控一切：监控软件交付过程的所有方面，包括应用程序性能、基础架构健康状况和用户体验。

强调安全性：安全性应该从一开始就构建到软件交付过程中，重点是持续的安全测试和监控。

结论

通过遵循 DevOps 的原则和最佳实践，组织可以释放 DevOps 的全部潜力并获得它提供的好处。 重要的是要注意，采用 DevOps 不是一次性事件，而是一个不断学习和改进的过程。 组织应该愿意接受变革并迭代其流程，以通过 DevOps 取得成功。

总之，DevOps 是一种将开发和运营团队聚集在一起协作工作的方法，共同关注为客户提供价值。 它建立在一套强调文化、自动化、衡量、共享和持续改进的原则之上。 采用 DevOps 提供了一系列好处，包括更快的上市时间、改进的质量、增强的协作、更有效地利用资源以及更高的客户满意度。 为了充分利用 DevOps，组织应该遵循一组强调自动化、版本控制、持续集成和交付、监控和安全性的最佳实践。 通过采用 DevOps 并不断迭代其流程，组织可以释放这种强大方法的全部潜力，并在交付软件方面取得成功。



原文:

DevOps is a methodology that enables organizations to deliver software faster and with higher quality. The term "DevOps" is a combination of "development" and "operations," and it refers to a set of practices that bring together the development and operations teams to work in collaboration, with a shared focus on delivering value to customers. In this article, we'll cover everything you need to know about DevOps, including its history, principles, benefits, and best practices.

History of DevOps

DevOps emerged in the early 2000s as a response to the challenges of software development at scale. Traditional development practices were characterized by long release cycles, siloed teams, and a lack of collaboration between developers and operations. This resulted in a slow and error-prone software delivery process, with frequent delays and bugs. DevOps was born as a way to address these challenges by bringing together the development and operations teams and creating a culture of collaboration and continuous improvement.

Principles of DevOps

DevOps is built on a set of principles that guide the way teams work together to deliver software. These principles are:

Culture: DevOps emphasizes a culture of collaboration, communication, and continuous improvement. Teams work together to break down silos and create a shared sense of ownership and accountability for delivering software.

Automation: DevOps relies on automation to streamline the software delivery process and reduce manual errors. Automated testing, deployment, and monitoring help teams deliver software faster and with higher quality.

Measurement: DevOps teams use data and metrics to measure the performance of their software delivery process and identify areas for improvement. This includes metrics like lead time, deployment frequency, and mean time to recovery.

Sharing: DevOps teams share knowledge, tools, and processes to accelerate learning and improve efficiency. This includes creating a shared knowledge base, using common tools and platforms, and sharing best practices.

Continuous improvement: DevOps is an iterative process that emphasizes continuous improvement. Teams are always looking for ways to optimize their software delivery process and reduce waste.

Benefits of DevOps

DevOps offers a number of benefits to organizations that adopt it. These benefits include:

Faster time to market: DevOps enables teams to deliver software faster by streamlining the software delivery process and reducing cycle times.

**Improved quality: DevOps **teams use automated testing and monitoring to detect and fix bugs earlier in the software delivery process, resulting in higher-quality software.

Increased collaboration: DevOps brings together the development and operations teams, creating a culture of collaboration and shared ownership for delivering software.

More efficient use of resources: DevOps reduces waste by streamlining the software delivery process and automating repetitive tasks.

Better customer satisfaction: DevOps enables organizations to deliver software that meets customer needs and expectations more quickly and reliably.

Best Practices of DevOps

To get the most out of DevOps, organizations should follow a set of best practices. These practices include:

Automate everything: Automate as much of the software delivery process as possible, including testing, deployment, and monitoring.

Use version control: Use a version control system to manage code changes and collaborate more effectively.

Implement continuous integration and delivery: Use continuous integration and delivery to automate the software delivery process and reduce cycle times.

Monitor everything: Monitor all aspects of the software delivery process, including application performance, infrastructure health, and user experience.

Emphasize security: Security should be built into the software delivery process from the start, with a focus on continuous security testing and monitoring.

Conclusion

DevOps is a powerful methodology that enables organizations to deliver software faster, with higher quality, and with greater collaboration between teams. By following the principles and best practices of DevOps, organizations can unlock the full potential of DevOps and achieve the benefits it offers. It's important to note that adopting DevOps is not a one-time event but a continuous journey of learning and improvement. Organizations should be willing to embrace change and iterate on their processes to achieve success with DevOps.

In summary, DevOps is a methodology that brings together development and operations teams to work in collaboration, with a shared focus on delivering value to customers. It is built on a set of principles that emphasize culture, automation, measurement, sharing, and continuous improvement. Adopting DevOps offers a range of benefits, including faster time to market, improved quality, increased collaboration, more efficient use of resources, and better customer satisfaction. To get the most out of DevOps, organizations should follow a set of best practices that emphasize automation, version control, continuous integration and delivery, monitoring, and security. By embracing DevOps and continuously iterating on their processes, organizations can unlock the full potential of this powerful methodology and achieve success in delivering software.

item.title: 如何自定义 .NET MAUI DataGrid 控件的外观 | How to Customize the Appearance of the .NET MAUI DataGrid Control

item.title: 开发者周的最新消息 | Latest news of the devs week
item.description:
亚历克斯真棒

发表于 5 月 8 日

我们认为 Slack 在生成 AI 方面具有独特的优势。 首先，也是最重要的，我们拥有大量的机构知识：在您使用 Slack 的这些年里，您的整个团队在渠道中所做的一切，都在您的档案中，一切都在您的指尖。 这是我们可以利用的巨大知识体系。
鉴于我们都依赖基于聊天的协作，利用生成人工智能来总结和自动化我们现在必须做的事情——劳动密集型滚动、从聊天流切换到聊天流、在那些无尽的线程中发现关键人物——这些类型生成的初始应用是有益的进步

以前，用户是通过哈希前面的名称和四个数字来标识的。
新系统将要求人们在“@”符号后创建一个唯一的用户名，使其与 Twitter、Instagram 和其他平台保持一致。

是时候开始discord names抢注生意了

全文

微软将农场押注于生成人工智能

微软正在开始 Bing 的下一阶段发展，重点放在 AI 集成上。 最近在纽约市举行的预览活动中，微软高管展示了将在未来几天、几周和几个月内添加到 Bing 的各种功能。 这些增强功能建立在 Bing 的最新开发成果之上，例如 AI 驱动的聊天机器人 Bing Chat，它利用了 OpenAI 的 GPT-4 和 DALL-E 2 模型。 未来，Bing 将变得更加以视觉为导向，将基于图像和图形的响应纳入 Bing Chat。 此外，它将使用户能够导出他们的 Bing Chat 历史记录并集成来自第三方插件的内容，从而提供更加个性化的体验。 苹果和谷歌合作制定了一项开创性的行业提案，旨在防止滥用蓝牙定位跟踪设备进行不必要的跟踪。 这一独特的规范将使此类设备能够在 iOS 和 Android 平台上兼容未经授权的跟踪检测和警报。 三星、Tile、Chipolo、eufy Security 和 Pebblebee 等公司已表示支持该规范草案，该规范为希望将这些功能纳入其产品的制造商提供了最佳实践和指南

这个新的行业规范建立在 AirTag 保护的基础上，通过与谷歌的合作，向前迈出了关键的一步，以帮助打击跨 iOS 和 Android 的不必要的跟踪。”

Alex Awesome

Posted on May 8

Slack is developing new generative AI capabilities, which will offer users automatic conversation summaries and native AI-powered writing assistance within its collaboration application.

Ali Rayl, Slack’s vice president of customer experience said:

We think that Slack has a unique advantage when it comes to generative AI. First and foremost, we hold an enormous amount of institutional knowledge: everything that your entire team has done in channels for the years that you've been using Slack, it's in your archives, it's all at your fingertips. That's an enormous body of knowledge for us to tap into.
Given the reliance we all have on chat-based collaboration, leveraging generative AI to summarize and automate what we have to do now — labor intensive scrolling around, switching from chat stream to chat stream, uncovering the key people in those endless threads — these types of initial applications of generative are helpful advancements

Link to the full article

Discord declared that all users on the platform will be requested to modify their usernames in the upcoming weeks.

Previously, users were identified by a name preceding a hash and four numbers.
The new system will require people to create a unique username following an "@" symbol, bringing it in line with Twitter, Instagram and other platforms.

It's time to start discord names squatting business

Full article

Microsoft are betting the farm on generative AI

Microsoft is commencing the next stage of Bing's growth, with a strong emphasis on AI integration. During a recent preview event in New York City, Microsoft executives showcased various features that will be added to Bing in the coming days, weeks, and months. These enhancements build upon Bing's recent developments, such as the AI-powered chatbot, Bing Chat, which utilizes OpenAI's GPT-4 and DALL-E 2 models. With over 100 million daily active users, Bing has facilitated more than half a billion chats and generated over 200 million images.

In the future, Bing will become more visually-oriented, incorporating image- and graphic-based responses in Bing Chat. Additionally, it will offer a more personalized experience by enabling users to export their Bing Chat histories and integrate content from third-party plugins. Bing Chat will also support multimodality, as it will be capable of answering queries in the context of images.

Full article

Apple and Google have partnered to address the issue of unwanted tracking through Bluetooth trackers like AirTags and Tile devices, aiming to combat stalking and other malicious uses

Apple and Google have collaborated on a pioneering industry proposal aimed at preventing the misuse of Bluetooth location-tracking devices for unwanted tracking purposes. This unique specification will enable such devices to be compatible with unauthorized tracking detection and alerts on both iOS and Android platforms. Companies like Samsung, Tile, Chipolo, eufy Security, and Pebblebee have expressed their support for the draft specification, which provides best practices and guidelines for manufacturers who wish to incorporate these features into their products

“Apple launched AirTag to give users the peace of mind knowing where to find their most important items,” said Ron Huang, Apple’s vice president of Sensing and Connectivity. “We built AirTag and the Find My network with a set of proactive features to discourage unwanted tracking — a first in the industry — and we continue to make improvements to help ensure the technology is being used as intended. This new industry specification builds upon the AirTag protections, and through collaboration with Google results in a critical step forward to help combat unwanted tracking across iOS and Android.”

item.title: DMK酵素疗法 | DMK Enzyme Therapy
item.description:
参宿四

发表于 5 月 8 日

由于能够解决各种皮肤问题，包括痤疮、老化和色素沉着，它在护肤行业广受欢迎。

什么是 DMK 酵素疗法？

该疗法由生物化学家和护肤专家 Danné Montague-King 开发，由于能够解决各种皮肤问题，包括痤疮、衰老和色素沉着，因此在护肤行业广受欢迎。

酶是天然存在的蛋白质，在人体的新陈代谢过程中起着至关重要的作用。 在 DMK 酵素疗法中，使用特定的酵素来促进细胞活动并恢复皮肤自然功能的平衡。 这些酶通过分解死皮细胞、刺激血液流动和增加皮肤的氧合作用而发挥作用。

例如，蛋白酶可以分解皮肤中的蛋白质，有助于去除死皮细胞并促进细胞更新。 另一方面，脂肪酶分解脂肪和油脂，使它们可用于治疗油性和易长粉刺的皮肤。 治疗中使用的其他酶包括水解酶、淀粉酶和纤维素酶，每种酶都有其特定的益处。

敷上一层厚厚的面膜，让客户放松约 45 分钟，让酵素发挥它们的魔力。 在此期间，酶会分解死皮细胞，刺激血液流动，并增加皮肤的氧合作用。

该疗法可以根据个人皮肤类型和问题进行定制，使其成为治疗痤疮、衰老、色素沉着等的有效方法。 该疗法还有助于改善皮肤质地和肤色，使皮肤看起来更明亮、更光滑、更年轻。

例如，Prozyme 面膜非常适合容易长粉刺的皮肤，而 Enzyme Masque #1 则专为解决老化和色素沉着而设计。 治疗师会评估客户的皮肤，并根据他们的需要推荐最合适的面膜。 该疗法有助于改善皮肤质地和肤色，减少细纹和皱纹的出现，并促进更年轻、容光焕发的肤色。 该疗法还有助于改善皮肤水合作用，使其成为治疗干燥和脱水皮肤的有效方法。

除了立竿见影的效果外，DMK 酵素疗法还为皮肤带来长期的好处。 该疗法可刺激胶原蛋白的生成，从而有助于增强皮肤结构并改善其弹性。 反过来，这有助于减少细纹和皱纹的出现，并促进更年轻的肤色。

该疗法有助于去除死皮细胞、疏通毛孔并改善血液流动，从而有助于减少痤疮和其他皮肤问题的发生。
与任何皮肤治疗一样，DMK 酶疗法也存在潜在副作用的风险。 这些可能包括发红、发痒和轻度肿胀。 然而，这些副作用通常是暂时的，应该会在几个小时内消退。

为了尽量减少副作用的风险，必须仔细遵循治疗师的指示。 客户应在治疗前后至少 48 小时内避免使用任何刺激性护肤品或去角质产品。 客户外出时还应避免阳光照射并涂抹防晒霜。

患有活动性单纯疱疹、湿疹、牛皮癣或其他炎症性皮肤病的患者禁用该疗法。 最近接受过化学换肤或其他侵入性皮肤治疗的客户也应避免使用 DMK 酶疗法。

平均而言，每次治疗的费用从 250 美元到 400 美元不等。 然而，一些治疗师可能会根据他们的经验和地点收取或多或少的费用。

虽然 DMK 酶疗法可能比其他一些皮肤疗法更昂贵，但它为皮肤提供了许多好处。 该疗法在解决广泛的皮肤问题方面非常有效，对于那些希望改善皮肤健康和外观的人来说，这是一项有价值的投资。

该疗法通过促进细胞活动和恢复皮肤自然功能的平衡来发挥作用。 虽然 DMK 酶疗法存在潜在副作用的风险，但这些副作用通常是暂时的，可以通过仔细遵循治疗师的指示将其降至最低。 查看纽约DMK酵素疗法的服务

Betelgeizer

Posted on May 8

How does DMK Enzyme Therapy Work to Transform Your Skin

DMK Enzyme Therapy is a non-invasive skin treatment that uses natural enzymes to improve skin health and appearance. It has gained popularity in the skincare industry due to its ability to address a wide range of skin concerns, including acne, aging, and pigmentation.

What is DMK Enzyme Therapy?

DMK Enzyme Therapy is a revolutionary skin treatment that uses natural enzymes to improve skin health and appearance. Developed by Danné Montague-King, a biochemist and skincare expert, this therapy has gained immense popularity in the skincare industry due to its ability to address a wide range of skin concerns, including acne, aging, and pigmentation.

Enzymes are naturally occurring proteins that play a crucial role in the body's metabolic processes. In DMK Enzyme Therapy, specific enzymes are used to promote cellular activity and restore balance to the skin's natural functions. These enzymes work by breaking down dead skin cells, stimulating blood flow, and increasing oxygenation to the skin.

There are several types of enzymes used in DMK Enzyme Therapy, each with its unique functions. Protease enzymes, for example, break down proteins in the skin, helping to remove dead skin cells and promote cell turnover. Lipase enzymes, on the other hand, break down fats and oils, making them useful for treating oily and acne-prone skin. Other enzymes used in the therapy include hydrolase, amylase, and cellulase, each with its specific benefits.

How does DMK Enzyme Therapy work?

DMK Enzyme Therapy is a non-invasive skin treatment that involves the application of a specialized enzyme mask to the skin. The mask is applied in a thick layer, and the client is left to relax for around 45 minutes while the enzymes work their magic. During this time, the enzymes break down dead skin cells, stimulate blood flow, and increase oxygenation to the skin.

One of the main benefits of DMK Enzyme Therapy is its ability to address a wide range of skin concerns. The therapy can be customized to suit individual skin types and concerns, making it an effective treatment for acne, aging, pigmentation, and more. The therapy also helps to improve skin texture and tone, leaving the skin looking brighter, smoother, and more youthful.

DMK Enzyme Therapy can be customized to address specific skin concerns by selecting the appropriate enzyme mask. For example, the Prozyme mask is ideal for acne-prone skin, while the Enzyme Masque #1 is designed to address aging and pigmentation. The therapist will assess the client's skin and recommend the most appropriate mask for their needs. The benefits of DMK Enzyme Therapy

DMK Enzyme Therapy offers numerous benefits for the skin. The therapy helps to improve skin texture and tone, reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles, and promote a more youthful, radiant complexion. The therapy also helps to improve skin hydration, making it an effective treatment for dry and dehydrated skin.

In addition to its immediate benefits, DMK Enzyme Therapy also offers long-term benefits for the skin. The therapy stimulates collagen production, which helps to strengthen the skin's structure and improve its elasticity. This, in turn, helps to reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles and promote a more youthful complexion.

DMK Enzyme Therapy also helps to improve skin health by promoting cellular activity and restoring balance to the skin's natural functions. The therapy helps to remove dead skin cells, unclog pores, and improve blood flow, which can help to reduce the occurrence of acne and other skin concerns.
The potential side effects of DMK Enzyme Therapy

Like any skin treatment, DMK Enzyme Therapy carries the risk of potential side effects. These can include redness, itching, and mild swelling. However, these side effects are usually temporary and should subside within a few hours.

To minimize the risk of side effects, it is essential to follow the therapist's instructions carefully. Clients should avoid using any harsh skincare products or exfoliants for at least 48 hours before and after the treatment. Clients should also avoid sun exposure and wear sunscreen when going outside.

DMK Enzyme Therapy is not suitable for everyone. The therapy is contraindicated for clients with active herpes simplex, eczema, psoriasis, or other inflammatory skin conditions. Clients who have recently undergone chemical peels or other invasive skin treatments should also avoid DMK Enzyme Therapy.

The cost of DMK Enzyme Therapy

The cost of DMK Enzyme Therapy varies depending on the location and therapist. On average, the therapy can cost anywhere from $250 to $400 per session. However, some therapists may charge more or less depending on their experience and location.

While DMK Enzyme Therapy may be more expensive than some other skin treatments, it offers numerous benefits for the skin. The therapy is highly effective at addressing a wide range of skin concerns, making it a valuable investment for those looking to improve their skin health and appearance.

*Does DMK Enzyme therapy worth it? *

DMK Enzyme Therapy is a revolutionary skin treatment that uses natural enzymes to improve skin health and appearance. The therapy works by promoting cellular activity and restoring balance to the skin's natural functions. DMK Enzyme Therapy offers numerous benefits for the skin, including improved texture and tone, reduced fine lines and wrinkles, and a more youthful, radiant complexion.

While DMK Enzyme Therapy carries the risk of potential side effects, these are usually temporary and can be minimized by following the therapist's instructions carefully. DMK Enzyme Therapy is contraindicated for some clients, including those with active herpes simplex, eczema, psoriasis, or other inflammatory skin conditions.

The cost of DMK Enzyme Therapy varies depending on the location and therapist, but it offers excellent value for those looking to improve their skin health and appearance. DMK Enzyme Therapy is a highly effective treatment for a wide range of skin concerns, making it a viable option for anyone looking to transform their skin.

item.title: 联系表格 7 贝宝附加 | Contact Form 7 Paypal Add on
item.description:
米利纳克拉尼

发表于 5 月 8 日

它允许您创建自定义 PayPal 付款表格、设置 PayPal 付款选项，并直接从您的 WordPress 仪表板管理交易。

使用Contact Form 7 PayPal 附加组件，您可以轻松创建包含 PayPal 付款的联系表格，使客户付款和企业管理交易变得简单。

为什么要使用 Contact Form 7 PayPal 附加组件？

您可能出于多种原因考虑为您的企业使用 Contact Form 7 PayPal 附加组件。 这里有一些：

易于使用：Contact Form 7 PayPal 附加组件易于设置和使用，即使对于那些不懂技术的人也是如此。

灵活：该插件允许您自定义您的 PayPal 支付选项，包括货币、价格和运输细节。

安全：Contact Form 7 PayPal 附加组件使用 PayPal 的安全支付网关，确保所有交易安全可靠。

节省时间：借助 Contact Form 7 PayPal 附加组件，您可以直接从 WordPress 仪表板管理交易，从而节省时间和精力。

经济高效：Contact Form 7 PayPal 附加组件是一种将 PayPal 支付集成到您的网站中的经济高效方式，无需昂贵的第三方插件。

截图：




结论

如果您是使用 PayPal 作为支付网关的企业主， Contact Form 7 PayPal 附加组件是您的 WordPress 网站必备的插件。 它允许您创建自定义 PayPal 付款表格、设置 PayPal 付款选项，并直接从您的 WordPress 仪表板管理交易。 凭借其易用性、灵活性、安全性和成本效益，Contact Form 7 PayPal 附加组件是各种规模企业的绝佳选择。

The Contact Form 7 PayPal add-on is a plugin that enables you to integrate PayPal payments into your Contact Form 7 forms. It allows you to create custom PayPal payment forms, set up PayPal payment options, and manage transactions directly from your WordPress dashboard.

With the Contact Form 7 PayPal add-on, you can easily create contact forms that include PayPal payments, making it simple for customers to make payments and for businesses to manage transactions.

Why use the Contact Form 7 PayPal add-on?

There are several reasons why you might want to consider using the Contact Form 7 PayPal add-on for your business. Here are a few:

It's easy to use : The Contact Form 7 PayPal add-on is simple to set up and use, even for those who are not tech-savvy.

It's flexible : The add-on allows you to customize your PayPal payment options, including the currency, price, and shipping details.

It's secure : The Contact Form 7 PayPal add-on uses PayPal's secure payment gateway, ensuring that all transactions are safe and secure.

It saves time : With the Contact Form 7 PayPal add-on, you can manage transactions directly from your WordPress dashboard, saving you time and effort.

It's cost-effective : The Contact Form 7 PayPal add-on is a cost-effective way to integrate PayPal payments into your website, without the need for expensive third-party plugins.

ScreenShots :




Conclusion

If you're a business owner who uses PayPal as your payment gateway, the Contact Form 7 PayPal add-on is a must-have plugin for your WordPress website. It allows you to create custom PayPal payment forms, set up PayPal payment options, and manage transactions directly from your WordPress dashboard. With its ease of use, flexibility, security, and cost-effectiveness, the Contact Form 7 PayPal add-on is an excellent choice for businesses of all sizes.

item.title: 如何在 Celo Alfajores 网络上使用 Nest.js 在后端铸造代币 | How to mint tokens in the backend using Nest.js on Celo Alfajores network
item.description:
杰里穆萨加

发表于 5 月 8 日

在本教程中，您将学习如何

  • 从具有薄荷功能的 openzeppelin 向导创建 ERC20 令牌智能合约
  • 将合约部署到 Celo 上的 Alfajore 测试网
  • 创建 API 以从已部署的智能合约获取方法到后端
  • 使用 celo-ethers-wrapper 使以太与 celo 网络兼容

先决条件

  • 要跟进本教程，需要对 API 和nestjs有基本的了解。

从 Hardhat 生成智能合约

  • 打开您的终端并使用此命令创建一个空文件夹mkdir my-project && cd my-project然后运行yarn init -y来初始化项目，然后使用命令yarn add --dev hardhat安装 hardhat。

  • 之后，运行yarn hardhat并选择 Create a typescript project 并将所有其他选项保留为默认值。

  • 在visual studio code或任何其他文本编辑器上打开项目文件夹。 它应该看起来像这样

  • 在 contracts 文件夹中，删除 Lock.sol 文件，同时删除 scripts 和 tests 文件夹中的文件。

  • 接下来，我们将使用 openzeppelin 合约库生成 ERC20 代币，而不是编写代码
    划痕。 但首先，我们需要在终端上使用此命令yarn add @openzeppelin/contracts安装它。 安装后，转到openzeppelin 向导为您的令牌生成合约。 选择 mintable 后，它应该是这样的

  • 在文本编辑器的 contracts 文件夹中，创建一个名为 MyToken.sol 的新文件，然后从 openzeppelin 向导中复制代码并将其粘贴到新创建的文件中。 你的文件应该是这样的
// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT pragma solidity ^0.8.9; import "@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC20/ERC20.sol"; import "@openzeppelin/contracts/access/Ownable.sol"; contract MyToken is ERC20, Ownable { constructor() ERC20("MyToken", "MTK") {} function mint(address to, uint256 amount) public onlyOwner { _mint(to, amount); } }

  • 合约中的 mint 函数用于创建该令牌的总供应量到函数中指定的钱包地址

  • 这是一个基本的 solidity 智能合约，具有我们可以在后端使用的 GET 和 POST 请求的功能。

  • 在您的终端上，运行命令yarn hardhat compile以编译智能合约并生成 artefact 文件夹，这是包含有关智能合约的有用信息的 JSON 文件。 我们将需要此 JSON 对象来与合约进行后端交互。

将智能合约部署到 Alfajores 网络

我们将使用安全帽通过在本地环境中设置 celo 来部署我们的智能合约。

  • 在您的文本编辑器中，打开 hardhat.config.ts 文件并在下面添加此 celo 配置代码
const config: HardhatUserConfig = { defaultNetwork: "alfajores", networks: { alfajores: { url: "https://alfajores-forno.celo-testnet.org", accounts: { mnemonic: process.env.MNEMONIC, path: "m/44'/52752'/0'/0" }, } }, solidity: "0.8.18", };
  • 在您的项目根目录中，创建一个 .env 文件，我们将在其中存储助记符。 它应该看起来像这样
MNEMONICS = "paste your 12 mnemonic keys here"
  • 接下来，使用此命令安装 dotenv yarn add dotenv以便我们可以访问配置文件中的助记符变量。 然后将dotenv导入文件
import * as dotenv from 'dotenv'; dotenv.config()
  • 你的 hardhat.config.ts 文件应该是这样的
import { HardhatUserConfig } from "hardhat/config"; import "@nomicfoundation/hardhat-toolbox"; import * as dotenv from 'dotenv'; dotenv.config() const config: HardhatUserConfig = { defaultNetwork: "alfajores", networks: { alfajores: { url: "https://alfajores-forno.celo-testnet.org", accounts: { mnemonic: process.env.MNEMONIC, path: "m/44'/52752'/0'/0" }, } }, solidity: "0.8.18", }; export default config;
  • 要部署我们的智能合约，请转到您的脚本文件夹，创建一个文件并将其命名为 deploy.ts ，将下面的代码片段复制并粘贴到其中
import { ethers } from "hardhat"; import { MyToken__factory } from "../typechain-types"; async function main() { const [signer] = await ethers.getSigners(); console.log("Deploying contracts with the account:", signer.address); console.log("Account balance:", (await signer.getBalance()).toString()); //get the wallet balance of the signer account const contractFactory = new MyToken__factory(signer); console.log(`Deploying MyToken Contract...\n`); const contract = await contractFactory.deploy(); const contractTxnReceipt = await contract.deployTransaction.wait(); console.log(`Deployed contract at address ${contractTxnReceipt.contractAddress}`); } main().catch((error) => { console.error(error); process.exitCode = 1; });
  • 在您的终端上运行此命令以部署合约yarn hardhat run scripts/deploy.ts --network alfajores ，您应该会得到类似于此的输出

  • 如果您收到显示 gas 不足的错误消息，请将您的地址复制到celo 水龙头并使用测试代币为您的帐户注资。

  • 记下部署的合约地址，因为它在对后端进行 API 调用时很有用

使用 Nest.js 设置后端

让我们开始吧

  • 在您的终端中，运行此命令npm i -g @nestjs/cli以全局安装 nest cli。
  • 在您的 my-project 目录中，运行nest new backend这将创建一个包含 Nest 文件基本结构的新文件夹。 使用 yarn 安装后，您的终端应如下所示

  • 在终端中，使用命令cd backend导航到后端文件夹并运行yarn start:dev以启动服务器并使其保持监视模式。

  • 打开你的浏览器并运行http://localhost:3000你会看到 Hello World! 的输出，这意味着服务器已经成功运行。

  • 让我们安装一个有助于描述我们的 RESTful API 的包nestjs/swagger 。 运行命令yarn add @nestjs/swagger进行安装。

  • 安装过程完成后，在您的 src 文件夹中，打开 main.ts 文件并将下面的代码粘贴到其中

import { NestFactory } from '@nestjs/core'; import { SwaggerModule, DocumentBuilder } from '@nestjs/swagger'; import { AppModule } from './app.module'; async function bootstrap() { const app = await NestFactory.create(AppModule); const config = new DocumentBuilder() .setTitle('Mint ERC20 Token on Celo Blockchain') .setDescription('The ERC20 API description') .setVersion('1.0') .addTag('celo, alfajores testnet') .build(); const document = SwaggerModule.createDocument(app, config); SwaggerModule.setup('api', app, document); await app.listen(3000); } bootstrap();
  • 打开浏览器并输入 url http://localhost:3000/api 。 你会得到这样的输出

将智能合约集成到后端

  • 我们将使用ethers库。 运行命令yarn add ethers@^5.7.2安装 ethers。
  • 还记得我们编译智能合约后生成的 artefact 文件夹吗？ 我们会将 MyToken.json 文件中的内容复制到我们的后端文件夹中。
  • 在backend/src文件夹中，新建一个名为 assets 的文件夹并新建一个文件 HelloWorld.json，将复制的内容粘贴到该文件中。
  • 打开 app.service.ts，导入 ethers 和 json 对象。 将下面的代码复制并粘贴到文件中
import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common'; import { ethers } from 'ethers'; import * as HelloWorldJson from './assets/HelloWorld.json'; @Injectable() export class AppService { }
  • 我们在导入 Json 时遇到问题，因此我们必须通过将"resolveJsonModule": true添加到 tsconfig.json 文件来解决该问题。

  • 记住我们从 hardhat 中的脚本文件部署智能合约后获得的合约地址，我们将使用该地址与该地址的功能进行交互。 现在，让我们将地址存储在 app.service.ts 文件中。 该文件应如下所示
import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common'; import { ethers } from 'ethers'; import * as MyTokenJson from './assets/MyToken.json'; const CONTRACT_ADDRESS = "0xBEc9321DEDcB18e954cf63BF81A6688bA5E4415E" // paste your own contract address gotten from the terminal @Injectable() export class AppService { getContractAddress(): string { return CONTRACT_ADDRESS; } }
  • 打开你的 app.controller.ts 文件，复制并粘贴下面的代码
import { Controller, Get } from '@nestjs/common'; import { AppService } from './app.service'; @Controller() export class AppController { constructor(private readonly appService: AppService) {} @Get('/contract-address') getContractAddress(): string { return this.appService.getContractAddress(); } }
  • 在浏览器中刷新 swagger UI 选项卡。 你会看到获取合约地址的路径。 执行它，你将得到如下图所示的 200 状态响应

  • 接下来，我们将使用我们安装的 ethers 库，以便我们可以与合约地址进行交互。

  • 首先，我们将获得总供应量，这是从 ERC20 代币生成的默认方法。

  • 在您的 app.controller.ts 文件中，添加一个名为 /total-suppy 的新路由，这将是一个异步操作，返回一个承诺。 更新后的文件将类似于下面的代码

import { Controller, Get } from '@nestjs/common'; import { AppService } from './app.service'; @Controller() export class AppController { constructor(private readonly appService: AppService) {} @Get('/contract-address') getContractAddress(): string { return this.appService.getContractAddress(); } @Get('/total-supply') async getTotalSupply(): Promise<number> { return this.appService.getTotalSupply(); } }
  • 接下来，在您的 app.service.ts 中，我们将创建一个名为 getTotalSupply 的异步函数。 我们将使用一个名为celo-ethers-wrapper 的包来使 ethers.js 与 celo 网络兼容。 在项目的后端目录中，使用yarn add @celo-tools/celo-ethers-wrapper安装包。 使用以下代码更新您的 app.service.ts
 import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common'; import { ethers } from 'ethers'; import * as MyTokenJson from './assets/MyToken.json'; import { CeloProvider } from '@celo-tools/celo-ethers-wrapper' const CONTRACT_ADDRESS = "0xBEc9321DEDcB18e954cf63BF81A6688bA5E4415E" // paste your own contract address gotten from the terminal @Injectable() export class AppService { getContractAddress(): string { return CONTRACT_ADDRESS; } async getTotalSupply(): Promise<number> { // Connecting to Alfajores testnet const provider = new CeloProvider('https://alfajores-forno.celo-testnet.org') await provider.ready const contract = new ethers.Contract(CONTRACT_ADDRESS, MyTokenJson.abi, provider ); return contract.totalSupply(); } }
  • 刷新浏览器上的 swagger UI 选项卡。 执行 /total-supply 路线后，你应该得到这样的输出

我们得到的返回值是一个大数。 我们如何将其转换为字符串？ 使用以下代码行更新您的 getTotalSupply 异步函数

const totalSupplyBigNumber = await contract.totalSupply(); const totalSupplyString = ethers.utils.formatEther(totalSupplyBigNumber); const totalSupplyNumber = parseFloat(totalSupplyString); return totalSupplyNumber;
  • 前往浏览器中的 swagger UI 选项卡，刷新页面并执行总供应路线。 您将在响应正文中获得 0 的返回值，因为我们没有在合约上铸造任何令牌。

  • 铸造代币需要签名者签署交易，并且签名者必须是合约的所有者或部署者。 我们将使用celo-ethers-wrapper中的 celoWallet 来签署交易和拨打电话。 要在后端铸造代币，我们需要发出 POST 请求。 在 app.controller.ts 文件中，我们需要先导入 Post 并创建一个名为 mintTokens 的新异步方法路由。 你更新的文件应该是这样的
import { Controller, Get, Post } from '@nestjs/common'; import { AppService } from './app.service'; @Controller() export class AppController { constructor(private readonly appService: AppService) {}m @Get('/contract-address') getContractAddress(): string { return this.appService.getContractAddress(); } @Get('/total-supply') async getTotalSupply(): Promise<number> { return this.appService.getTotalSupply(); } @Post('/mint-tokens') async mintTokens(){ return this.appService.mintTokens(); } }
  • 在 app.service.ts 文件中，从 celo-ethers-wrapper 导入 CeloWallet 并在后端目录中创建一个 .env 文件，该文件存储您用于部署智能合约的帐户的私钥，因为这是签名者帐户。 确保使用yarn add dotenv安装 dotenv。 您更新后的文件应如下所示
import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common'; import { ethers } from 'ethers'; import * as MyTokenJson from './assets/MyToken.json'; import { CeloProvider } from '@celo-tools/celo-ethers-wrapper' import { CeloWallet } from "@celo-tools/celo-ethers-wrapper"; import * as dotenv from 'dotenv'; dotenv.config() const CONTRACT_ADDRESS = "0xBEc9321DEDcB18e954cf63BF81A6688bA5E4415E" // paste your own contract address gotten from the terminal const WALLET_ADDRESS = "0x122851EB3915cc769dECBf95a566e7fC8aAc2125" // paste your own wallet address @Injectable() export class AppService { getContractAddress(): string { return CONTRACT_ADDRESS; } async getTotalSupply(): Promise<number> { // Connecting to Alfajores testnet const provider = new CeloProvider('https://alfajores-forno.celo-testnet.org') await provider.ready const contract = new ethers.Contract(CONTRACT_ADDRESS, MyTokenJson.abi, provider ); const totalSupplyBigNumber = await contract.totalSupply(); const totalSupplyString = ethers.utils.formatEther(totalSupplyBigNumber); const totalSupplyNumber = parseFloat(totalSupplyString); return totalSupplyNumber; } async mintTokens(){ // Connecting to Alfajores testnet const provider = new CeloProvider('https://alfajores-forno.celo-testnet.org') await provider.ready const wallet = new CeloWallet(process.env.PRIVATE_KEY, provider); const contract = new ethers.Contract(CONTRACT_ADDRESS, MyTokenJson.abi, wallet ); const mint = await contract.mint(WALLET_ADDRESS, 100000); return mint; } }
  • 在你的 swagger UI 选项卡中，刷新它并执行 mintTokens 路由，这是一个 POST 请求。 你会得到这样的输出

这表示 POST 请求成功并且您的令牌是根据您在 mintFunction 方法中提供的参数生成的。

  • 接下来，让我们通过执行 /total-supply GET 请求来检查我们拥有的代币数量。 根据执行 mintTokens POST 请求的次数，您应该会得到这样的输出。

在我的响应主体中，我得到了 3e+13 的值，这是 3*10^13 的 E- 表示法版本。

您可以通过在celo alfajores explorer中搜索合约地址来查看合约的详细信息

这是我的合约地址0xbec9321dedcb18e954cf63bf81a6688ba5e4415e的交易详情。

  • 本教程到此结束，我希望您从本教程中学到一两个知识，并且它以某种方式对您有所帮助。 让我知道你的想法。 干杯

一旦暂停，jerrymusaga 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，jerrymusaga 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，jerrymusaga 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 jerrymusaga 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 jerrymusaga 的方法：

降低 jerrymusaga 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 jerrymusaga 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Jerry Musaga

Posted on May 8

In this tutorial, you will learn how to

  • create an ERC20 token smart contract from openzeppelin wizard with mint functionality
  • deploy the contract to the Alfajore's testnet on Celo
  • create API to fetch methods from the deployed smart contract to the backend
  • use the celo-ethers-wrapper to make ethers compatible with celo network

Prerequisite

  • To follow up this tutorial, a basic understanding on API and nestjs is necessary.

Generate smart contract from Hardhat

  • Open up your terminal and create an empty folder using this command mkdir my-project && cd my-project Then run yarn init -y to initialize the project and then install hardhat using the command yarn add --dev hardhat.

  • After that, run yarn hardhat and select Create a typescript project and leave every other option as the default.

  • Open up the project folder on visual studio code or any other text editor. It should look like this

  • In the contracts folder, delete the Lock.sol file and do so with the files in the scripts and tests folder too.

  • Next up, we will be using the openzeppelin contract library to generate the ERC20 token, instead of writing the code from
    scratch. But first, we need to install it using this command yarn add @openzeppelin/contracts on the terminal. After the installation, go to openzeppelin wizard to generate the contract for your token. After selecting mintable, it should look like this

  • On your text editor, in your contracts folder, create a new file called MyToken.sol and copy the codes from the openzeppelin wizard and paste it into the newly created file. Your file should look like this 
// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
pragma solidity ^0.8.9;

import "@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC20/ERC20.sol";
import "@openzeppelin/contracts/access/Ownable.sol";

contract MyToken is ERC20, Ownable {
    constructor() ERC20("MyToken", "MTK") {}

    function mint(address to, uint256 amount) public onlyOwner {
        _mint(to, amount);
    }
}

  • The mint function in your contract is for creating the total supply of that token to the wallet address specified in the function

  • This is a basic solidity smart contract with the functionality of a GET and POST requests that we can use in the backend.

  • On your terminal, run the command yarn hardhat compile so as to compile the smart contract and generate the artefact folder, which is the JSON file containing useful informations about the smart contract. We will need this JSON object for the backend interaction with the contract.

Deploy smart contract to Alfajores network

We will be using hardhat to deploy our smart contract by setting up celo in our local environment.

  • In your text editor, open up hardhat.config.ts file and add up this celo configuration code below 
const config: HardhatUserConfig = {
  defaultNetwork: "alfajores",
  networks: {
    alfajores: {
      url: "https://alfajores-forno.celo-testnet.org",
      accounts: {
        mnemonic: process.env.MNEMONIC,
        path: "m/44'/52752'/0'/0"
      },
    }
  },
  solidity: "0.8.18",
};
  • In your project root directory, create a .env file where we will be storing our mnemonics. It should look like this 
MNEMONICS = "paste your 12 mnemonic keys here"
  • Next up, install the dotenv using this command yarn add dotenv so we can access the mnemonic variable in the config file. And then import the dotenv into the file 
import * as dotenv from 'dotenv';
dotenv.config()
  • Your hardhat.config.ts file should look like this 
import { HardhatUserConfig } from "hardhat/config";
import "@nomicfoundation/hardhat-toolbox";

import * as dotenv from 'dotenv';
dotenv.config()

const config: HardhatUserConfig = {
  defaultNetwork: "alfajores",
  networks: {
    alfajores: {
      url: "https://alfajores-forno.celo-testnet.org",
      accounts: {
        mnemonic: process.env.MNEMONIC,
        path: "m/44'/52752'/0'/0"
      },
    }
  },
  solidity: "0.8.18",
};

export default config;
  • To deploy our smart contract, go to your scripts folder, create a file and name it deploy.ts , copy & paste the code snippets below into it 
import { ethers } from "hardhat";
import { MyToken__factory } from "../typechain-types";

async function main() {
  const [signer] =  await ethers.getSigners();
  console.log("Deploying contracts with the account:", signer.address);
  console.log("Account balance:", (await signer.getBalance()).toString()); //get the wallet balance of the signer account
  const contractFactory = new MyToken__factory(signer);

  console.log(`Deploying MyToken Contract...\n`);
  const contract = await contractFactory.deploy();
  const contractTxnReceipt = await contract.deployTransaction.wait();
  console.log(`Deployed contract at address ${contractTxnReceipt.contractAddress}`);


}

main().catch((error) => {
  console.error(error);
  process.exitCode = 1;
});
  • Run this command on your terminal to deploy the contract yarn hardhat run scripts/deploy.ts --network alfajores and you should get an output similar to this

  • If you get an error showing insufficient gas, copy your address to the celo faucet and fund your account with test tokens.

  • Take note of the deployed contract address because it will be useful when making API calls to the backend

Setting up the backend using Nest.js

Nest.js is a framework for building Node.js server side applications.
Let's get started

  • In your terminal, run this command npm i -g @nestjs/cli to install nest cli globally.
  • In your my-project directory, run nest new backend which will create a new folder consisting of the base structure of Nest files. After installation using yarn, your terminal should look like this

  • In the terminal, navigate into the backend folder using the command cd backend and run yarn start:dev to start up the server and keep it in watch mode.

  • Open up your browser and run http://localhost:3000 and you will see the output of Hello World!, which means the server is running successfully.

  • Let us install a package that will help in describing our RESTful APIs, nestjs/swagger. Run the command yarn add @nestjs/swagger to install.

  • After the installation process is complete, in your src folder, open up the main.ts file and paste the code below into it

import { NestFactory } from '@nestjs/core';
import { SwaggerModule, DocumentBuilder } from '@nestjs/swagger';
import { AppModule } from './app.module';

async function bootstrap() {
  const app = await NestFactory.create(AppModule);

  const config = new DocumentBuilder()
    .setTitle('Mint ERC20 Token on Celo Blockchain')
    .setDescription('The ERC20 API description')
    .setVersion('1.0')
    .addTag('celo, alfajores testnet')
    .build();
  const document = SwaggerModule.createDocument(app, config);
  SwaggerModule.setup('api', app, document);

  await app.listen(3000);
}
bootstrap();
  • Open up your browser and enter the url http://localhost:3000/api. You will get an output like this

Integrating Smart contract to the Backend

  • We will be using the ethers library. Run the command yarn add ethers@^5.7.2 to install ethers.
  • Remember the artefact folder we generated after we compiled the smart contract? We will copy the contents in MyToken.json file into our backend folder.
  • In the backend/src folder, create a new folder called assets and create a new file HelloWorld.json, paste the copied contents into the file.
  • Open up app.service.ts, import ethers and the json object. Copy and paste the code below into the file 
import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';
import { ethers } from 'ethers';
import * as HelloWorldJson from './assets/HelloWorld.json';

@Injectable()
export class AppService {

}
  • We have a problem in importing Json, so we have to fix that by adding "resolveJsonModule": true to tsconfig.json file.

  • Remember the contract address we got after deploying the smart contract from the script file in hardhat, we will be using that address to interact with the functions of that address. For now, let's store the address in the app.service.ts file. The file should look like this 
import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';
import { ethers } from 'ethers';
import * as MyTokenJson from './assets/MyToken.json';

const CONTRACT_ADDRESS = "0xBEc9321DEDcB18e954cf63BF81A6688bA5E4415E" // paste your own contract address gotten from the terminal

@Injectable()
export class AppService {
  getContractAddress(): string {
    return CONTRACT_ADDRESS;
  }
}
  • Open your app.controller.ts file, copy and paste the codes below 
import { Controller, Get } from '@nestjs/common';
import { AppService } from './app.service';

@Controller()
export class AppController {
  constructor(private readonly appService: AppService) {}

  @Get('/contract-address')
  getContractAddress(): string {
    return this.appService.getContractAddress();
  }
}
  • Refresh your swagger UI tab in your browser. You'll see the route to get contract address. Execute it and you will get 200 status response like the image below

  • Next up, we will be using the ethers library that we installed so we can interact with the contract address.

  • First up, we will get the total supply which is a default method generated from an ERC20 token.

  • In your app.controller.ts file, add a new route called /total-suppy, which will be an async operation, returning a promise. The updated file will look like the codes below

import { Controller, Get } from '@nestjs/common';
import { AppService } from './app.service';

@Controller()
export class AppController {
  constructor(private readonly appService: AppService) {}

  @Get('/contract-address')
  getContractAddress(): string {
    return this.appService.getContractAddress();
  }

  @Get('/total-supply')
  async getTotalSupply(): Promise<number> {
    return this.appService.getTotalSupply();
  }
}
  • Next up, in your app.service.ts, we will be creating an asynchronous function called getTotalSupply. We will be using a package called celo-ethers-wrapper to be able to make ethers.js to be compatible with the celo network. In your backend directory of your project, install the package using yarn add @celo-tools/celo-ethers-wrapper. Update your app.service.ts with the codes below 
import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';
import { ethers } from 'ethers';
import * as MyTokenJson from './assets/MyToken.json';
import { CeloProvider } from '@celo-tools/celo-ethers-wrapper'

const CONTRACT_ADDRESS = "0xBEc9321DEDcB18e954cf63BF81A6688bA5E4415E" // paste your own contract address gotten from the terminal

@Injectable()
export class AppService {
  getContractAddress(): string {
    return CONTRACT_ADDRESS;
  }

  async getTotalSupply(): Promise<number> {
    // Connecting to Alfajores testnet
    const provider = new CeloProvider('https://alfajores-forno.celo-testnet.org')
    await provider.ready
    const contract = new ethers.Contract(CONTRACT_ADDRESS, MyTokenJson.abi, provider );
    return contract.totalSupply();
  }
}
  • Refresh your swagger UI tab on your browser. After executing your /total-supply route, you should get an output like this

The return value we get is a Big Number. How do we convert it to a string? Update your getTotalSupply async function with the lines of code below

const totalSupplyBigNumber = await contract.totalSupply();
    const totalSupplyString = ethers.utils.formatEther(totalSupplyBigNumber);
    const totalSupplyNumber = parseFloat(totalSupplyString);
    return totalSupplyNumber;
  • Head to the swagger UI tab in browser, refresh the page and execute the total supply route. You'll get a return value of 0 at the response body because we have not minted any token on our contract.

  • Minting tokens requires a signer to sign the transaction and that signer has to be the owner or the deployer of the contract. We will use the celoWallet from the celo-ethers-wrapper to be able to sign transactions and make calls. To mint tokens in the backend, we will need to make a POST request. In the app.controller.ts file, we need to first import Post and create a new async method route called mintTokens. Your updated file should look like that 
import { Controller, Get, Post } from '@nestjs/common';
import { AppService } from './app.service';


@Controller()
export class AppController {
  constructor(private readonly appService: AppService) {}m

  @Get('/contract-address')
  getContractAddress(): string {
    return this.appService.getContractAddress();
  }

  @Get('/total-supply')
  async getTotalSupply(): Promise<number> {
    return this.appService.getTotalSupply();
  }

  @Post('/mint-tokens')
  async mintTokens(){
    return this.appService.mintTokens();
  }
}
  • In the app.service.ts file, import the CeloWallet from celo-ethers-wrapper and create an .env file in the backend directory which stores the private key of the account you used to deploy the smart contract because that is the signer account. Make sure you install dotenv with yarn add dotenv. Your updated file should look like this 
import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';
import { ethers } from 'ethers';
import * as MyTokenJson from './assets/MyToken.json';
import { CeloProvider } from '@celo-tools/celo-ethers-wrapper'
import { CeloWallet } from "@celo-tools/celo-ethers-wrapper";
import * as dotenv from 'dotenv';
dotenv.config()


const CONTRACT_ADDRESS = "0xBEc9321DEDcB18e954cf63BF81A6688bA5E4415E" // paste your own contract address gotten from the terminal
const WALLET_ADDRESS = "0x122851EB3915cc769dECBf95a566e7fC8aAc2125" // paste your own wallet address

@Injectable()
export class AppService {
  getContractAddress(): string {
    return CONTRACT_ADDRESS;
  }

  async getTotalSupply(): Promise<number> {
    // Connecting to Alfajores testnet
    const provider = new CeloProvider('https://alfajores-forno.celo-testnet.org')
    await provider.ready
    const contract = new ethers.Contract(CONTRACT_ADDRESS, MyTokenJson.abi, provider );
    const totalSupplyBigNumber = await contract.totalSupply();
    const totalSupplyString = ethers.utils.formatEther(totalSupplyBigNumber);
    const totalSupplyNumber = parseFloat(totalSupplyString);
    return totalSupplyNumber;
  }

  async mintTokens(){
    // Connecting to Alfajores testnet
    const provider = new CeloProvider('https://alfajores-forno.celo-testnet.org')
    await provider.ready
    const wallet = new CeloWallet(process.env.PRIVATE_KEY, provider);
    const contract = new ethers.Contract(CONTRACT_ADDRESS, MyTokenJson.abi, wallet );
    const mint = await contract.mint(WALLET_ADDRESS, 100000); 
    return mint;
  }
}
  • In your swagger UI tab, refresh it and execute the mintTokens route which is a POST request. You'll get an output like this

This indicates that the POST request was successful and your tokens are minted based on the parameter you supplied in your mintFunction method.

  • Next up, let's check the number of tokens we have by executing the /total-supply GET request. You should get an output like this base on the amount of times you execute the mintTokens POST request.

In my response body, I got the value of 3e+13 which is the E- notation version of 3*10^13.

You can view the details of your contract by searching up the contract address in the celo alfajores explorer

This is the transaction details from my contract address 0xbec9321dedcb18e954cf63bf81a6688ba5e4415e.

  • This is the end of this tutorial and I hope you learned one or two from this tutorial and it helped in one way or the other. Let me know what you think. Cheers

Micael Levi L. C. - Feb 5
